test_cases.title: Verify Settings menu navigation
test_cases.description:
Step 1: Press the GEAR/MENU button on the remote.
Expected: Settings menu/Quick Settings sidebar appears within 2 seconds.

Step 2: Navigate to 'All Settings' to open the full settings tree.
Expected: Full settings menu loads showing all top-level categories (Network, Picture, Sound, System, etc.).

Step 3: Navigate into each top-level category (Network, HDMI & Gaming, Accessibility, System, etc.) and verify sub-menus load.
Expected: Each category expands to show its sub-items without errors, freezes, or crashes.

Step 4: Navigate back using the Back button from each sub-menu.
Expected: Returns to parent menu correctly; no state corruption.

Step 5: Press Home to exit settings entirely.
Expected: Returns to Home screen; settings state is not persisted incorrectly.
test_cases.expected_output: All settings menu categories are navigable. Sub-menus load correctly. Back navigation works. No crashes or UI freezes during full menu traversal.

test_cases.title: Verify System Information display
test_cases.description:
Step 1: Navigate to Settings > System > System Information.
Expected: System Information screen loads.

Step 2: Verify the following fields are displayed: Model Name, Serial Number, Firmware Version, IP Address, MAC Address.
Expected: All fields are populated with valid values (non-empty, correct format).

Step 3: Press Menu/Settings button during OOBE on 'Choose a Language' screen.
Expected: TV Information overlay is accessible showing the same system details.

Step 4: Press Menu/Settings button during OOBE on 'Connect Your Network' screen.
Expected: TV Information is accessible and displays system details even before network connection.
test_cases.expected_output: System information is accessible from Settings and during OOBE contexts. All required fields (Model, Serial, FW Version, IP, MAC) display correctly.

test_cases.title: Verify Factory Reset execution
test_cases.description:
Step 1: Navigate to Settings > System > Reset to Factory Settings.
Expected: Reset menu appears showing options: Reset TV, Reset Apps, Restart, Cancel.

Step 2: Select 'Reset TV'.
Expected: Confirmation dialog appears warning all data will be erased.

Step 3: Confirm the factory reset.
Expected: TV begins reset process (progress indicator shown).

Step 4: Wait for reset to complete.
Expected: TV reboots and launches into OOBE (Choose a Language screen) within 3 minutes.

Step 5: Verify factory reset from the front panel button (press and hold reset button on TV body).
Expected: Same factory reset flow triggers from physical button.
test_cases.expected_output: Factory reset completes successfully from both menu and front panel. TV returns to OOBE. All user data cleared.

test_cases.title: Verify Sleep Timer functionality
test_cases.description:
Step 1: Navigate to Settings > System > Sleep Timer.
Expected: Sleep Timer options are shown (Off, 30 min, 1 hr, 2 hr, etc.).

Step 2: Set sleep timer to minimum value (e.g., 30 minutes).
Expected: Timer is set; confirmation shown.

Step 3: Verify system countdown UI message displays with timer icon.
Expected: When timer approaches expiry, TV displays a 30-second countdown notification.

Step 4: Let countdown expire.
Expected: TV powers off to standby after countdown completes.
test_cases.expected_output: Sleep timer can be set, displays countdown notification, and TV powers off on expiry.

test_cases.title: Verify Parental Controls access
test_cases.description:
Step 1: Navigate to Settings > System > Parental Controls.
Expected: PIN entry screen appears.

Step 2: Enter correct PIN.
Expected: Parental Controls menu opens showing rating locks, channel blocks, input locks.

Step 3: Attempt to access locked content without entering PIN.
Expected: PIN Required prompt appears blocking access.

Step 4: Enter incorrect PIN 3 times.
Expected: Lockout or delay mechanism activates.
test_cases.expected_output: PIN is required to access locked content/settings. Correct PIN grants access. Incorrect PIN blocks access with appropriate UI feedback.

test_cases.title: Verify Menu timeout and auto-dismissal behavior
test_cases.description:
Step 1: Open the Settings menu by pressing the GEAR/MENU button.
Expected: Settings menu appears.

Step 2: Leave the menu idle (no button presses) for 60 seconds.
Expected: Menu automatically closes/dismisses after 60 seconds of inactivity.

Step 3: Verify the TV returns to the previous content/state after menu dismissal.
Expected: Content resumes normally; no frozen or black screen.

Step 4: With a gaming console connected via HDMI, verify that the gaming console's MENU button does NOT dismiss or interfere with the TV Settings UI.
Expected: TV UI settings remain open; gaming console button presses do not close TV menu.

Step 5: Open Quick Settings menu and leave idle for 60 seconds.
Expected: Quick Settings also auto-dismisses after 60-second inactivity timeout.
test_cases.expected_output: Both Settings and Quick Settings menus auto-dismiss after 60 seconds of inactivity. Gaming console button presses do not interfere with TV settings UI.

test_cases.title: Verify Quick Settings menu behavior and dismissal paths
test_cases.description:
Step 1: Press MENU/GEAR button to open Quick Settings sidebar.
Expected: Quick Settings sidebar appears showing key menu items.

Step 2: Verify TV Name is displayed at the top of the Quick Settings menu.
Expected: TV Name appears (up to 24 characters).

Step 3: Dismiss Quick Settings by pressing MENU button again.
Expected: Menu closes, returns to content.

Step 4: Re-open Quick Settings, then dismiss by pressing Back button.
Expected: Menu closes.

Step 5: Re-open Quick Settings, then dismiss by pressing Home button.
Expected: Menu closes, Home screen appears.

Step 6: Re-open Quick Settings, then dismiss by pressing an app Hot-Key button.
Expected: Menu closes, corresponding app launches.

Step 7: Re-open Quick Settings, then dismiss by pressing Input button.
Expected: Menu closes, Input selection appears.

Step 8: Re-open Quick Settings, then dismiss by pressing Mic button.
Expected: Menu closes, voice assistant activates.

Step 9: While Quick Settings is open, press Channel Up/Down.
Expected: Quick Settings closes and channel changes.

Step 10: While Quick Settings is open, press Volume Up/Down.
Expected: Volume adjusts; Quick Settings may remain open or close per spec.

Step 11: Start playing content, open Quick Settings, verify it does not block pop-up ads or system notifications.
Expected: System pop-ups appear above/alongside Quick Settings overlay.
test_cases.expected_output: Quick Settings opens/closes via all specified button paths. TV Name shown (max 24 chars). Channel/Volume keys handled correctly. Pop-up ads not blocked.

test_cases.title: Verify Quick Settings content and online/offline states
test_cases.description:
Step 1: With network connected (online), open Quick Settings.
Expected: Quick Settings shows full menu items including network-dependent options.

Step 2: Verify all general menu items are present in Quick Settings (Picture, Sound, Network, Sleep Timer, etc.).
Expected: All expected menu items visible.

Step 3: Navigate to 'Get Connected' item in Quick Settings.
Expected: Navigating to this item provides a path to the Network connection screen.

Step 4: Disconnect network, then open Quick Settings (offline state).
Expected: Quick Settings adapts to show offline-appropriate items; network-dependent items are disabled/hidden.

Step 5: Verify Quick Settings can be re-opened after being dismissed.
Expected: Menu re-opens correctly each time without state corruption.
test_cases.expected_output: Quick Settings displays correct items for online/offline states. Network shortcut works. Menu re-opens reliably after dismissal.

test_cases.title: Verify Network Settings menu UI elements
test_cases.description:
Step 1: Navigate to Settings > Network.
Expected: Network settings menu loads showing current connection status and options.

Step 2: Verify 'My Networks' sub-section is present showing saved/connected networks.
Expected: My Networks displays current SSID or 'Not Connected'.

Step 3: Open 'More Access Points' list.
Expected: List of available WiFi networks appears.

Step 4: Verify there is no limit on the number of SSIDs displayed in 'More Access Points'.
Expected: All detected networks are listed.

Step 5: Verify the list auto-rescans while user is actively viewing the network menu.
Expected: Network list refreshes periodically; selector/highlight stays on the same SSID during refresh.

Step 6: Verify rescans only occur when user is actively looking at the network menu (not in background).
Expected: No background rescans when menu is closed.

Step 7: Navigate away from network menu and return.
Expected: Fresh scan occurs on re-entry.
test_cases.expected_output: Network menu shows connection status, My Networks section, and full SSID list. Auto-rescan works correctly with selector persistence. No background scanning.

test_cases.title: Verify WiFi connection flow via Settings menu (multi-band/encryption)
test_cases.description:
Step 1: Navigate to Settings > Network > More Access Points.
Expected: WiFi SSID list appears.

Step 2: Select a 2.4GHz WPA2 network, enter correct password.
Expected: Keyboard appears, password accepted, connection successful, keyboard closes, returns to Network settings.

Step 3: Select a 5GHz WPA2/WPA3 network, enter correct password.
Expected: Connection successful via System Menu for 5GHz band.

Step 4: Select a 6GHz WPA3-Personal SAE network, enter correct password.
Expected: Connection successful for 6GHz WPA3-SAE.

Step 5: Select a 6GHz WPA3-Enhanced Open network.
Expected: Connection successful for Enhanced Open.

Step 6: Select a 6GHz WPA2+WPA3 Mixed Mode network.
Expected: Connection successful for mixed mode.

Step 7: After successful connection, verify keyboard screen closes and Network settings page is displayed.
Expected: UI returns to Network settings showing connected status.
test_cases.expected_output: WiFi connection works from Settings menu for all band/encryption combinations (2.4/5/6 GHz, WPA2/WPA3/SAE/Enhanced Open/Mixed). Keyboard dismisses after successful connection.

test_cases.title: Verify network switching and error handling in Settings
test_cases.description:
Step 1: While connected to a WiFi network, navigate to Settings > Network > More Access Points.
Expected: Current and available networks displayed.

Step 2: Choose a different network, enter valid credentials.
Expected: TV disconnects from current network, connects to new network, and returns to Network settings showing new connection.

Step 3: Choose a different network, enter password with fewer than 8 characters.
Expected: Error notification displayed indicating password too short.

Step 4: Choose a different network, enter incorrect password (8+ chars).
Expected: Connection attempt fails; error notification shows 'Incorrect password' or similar message.

Step 5: Trigger WiFi disconnect from Settings > Network > Disconnect or equivalent.
Expected: WiFi disconnect diagnostic/confirmation appears.

Step 6: Verify error notification UI elements (icon, text, dismiss behavior).
Expected: Notifications are clearly readable, dismissible, and do not persist indefinitely.
test_cases.expected_output: Network switching works with valid credentials. Invalid credentials show appropriate error messages. Short passwords rejected. Disconnect diagnostic accessible.

test_cases.title: Verify Network Settings launch from troubleshooting screens
test_cases.description:
Step 1: Trigger a 'No Network' troubleshooting/galaxy screen (disconnect WiFi, attempt network-dependent action).
Expected: 'No Network' troubleshooting screen appears.

Step 2: Verify option to launch Network Settings is present on troubleshooting screen.
Expected: A button/link to open Network Settings is available.

Step 3: Tap/select the Network Settings option from troubleshooting.
Expected: Network settings menu opens directly from the troubleshooting context.

Step 4: Connect to a network from this flow.
Expected: Connection successful; troubleshooting screen resolves.

Step 5: Repeat from different troubleshooting entry points (SmartCast apps, streaming content errors, etc.).
Expected: Network Settings is accessible from all 'No Network' troubleshooting screens.
test_cases.expected_output: Network Settings is launchable from all 'No Network' troubleshooting/galaxy screens. User can connect to WiFi and resolve the issue from that flow.

test_cases.title: Verify Captive Portal and SoftAP network settings
test_cases.description:
Step 1: Set up a Captive Portal WiFi network in the test environment.
Expected: Network is broadcasting.

Step 2: Navigate to Settings > Network and verify the Captive Portal SSID appears in the network list.
Expected: Captive Portal SSID is listed alongside normal networks.

Step 3: Select the Captive Portal SSID from Menu > Network.
Expected: Captive Portal setup/authentication flow launches.

Step 4: Complete Captive Portal authentication.
Expected: TV connects through captive portal; connection status shows 'Connected'.

Step 5: Verify Manual SoftAP login supports settings from multiple platforms (Windows, macOS, Android, iOS).
Expected: SoftAP configuration accepts connections from all tested platforms.
test_cases.expected_output: Captive Portal SSIDs appear in network list. Captive Portal auth flow works from Settings. SoftAP login supports multi-platform access.

test_cases.title: Verify Network Security Type and DHCP Settings menus
test_cases.description:
Step 1: Navigate to Settings > Network > Network Security Type.
Expected: Menu appears showing header text and help description.

Step 2: Verify the list of available security types displayed (WPA2, WPA3, Open, etc.).
Expected: All supported security types listed correctly.

Step 3: Navigate to Settings > Network > DHCP Settings.
Expected: DHCP Settings menu opens.

Step 4: Verify DHCP in default mode (Auto/On).
Expected: IP Address, Subnet, Gateway, DNS fields shown with auto-acquired values.

Step 5: Switch DHCP to Off mode.
Expected: Manual IP entry fields become editable.

Step 6: Switch DHCP to ManualDNS mode.
Expected: Only DNS fields become editable; IP/Subnet/Gateway remain auto-acquired.
test_cases.expected_output: Network Security Type menu shows correct options with help text. DHCP Settings correctly displays Auto, Off, and ManualDNS modes with appropriate editable fields.

test_cases.title: Verify OOBE launch from OSD menu and settings access during OOBE
test_cases.description:
Step 1: From normal TV operation, navigate to Settings > System > Reset to Factory Settings or OSD Menu option to launch OOBE.
Expected: OOBE can be re-launched from the OSD menu.

Step 2: During OOBE on 'Choose a Language' screen, press the Menu key.
Expected: Side bar menu or TV Info overlay appears.

Step 3: Verify TV Information is accessible by pressing settings/menu button during OOBE.
Expected: System information (model, firmware, etc.) is viewable during OOBE without exiting the wizard.

Step 4: Back out of OOBE from 'Choose a Language' screen, launch Side Bar menu.
Expected: User Manual or help content is accessible from the sidebar.

Step 5: During OOBE on 'Connect to a WiFi Network' screen, tap 'Skip Network' / 'Setup Network Later'.
Expected: Updated text message confirms skip and OOBE proceeds to next step.

Step 6: Verify the 'Set Up Later' text messaging is correct and consistent.
Expected: Text matches specification across all skip scenarios.
test_cases.expected_output: OOBE can be launched from OSD menu. Menu/Settings key works during OOBE to show TV Info. Skip Network option works with correct messaging. Sidebar accessible during OOBE.

test_cases.title: Verify HDMI & Gaming Settings menu options
test_cases.description:
Step 1: Navigate to Settings > HDMI & Gaming.
Expected: HDMI & Gaming menu opens showing HDMI Settings sub-menu.

Step 2: Open HDMI Settings and verify the following options are present and accessible: CEC, CEC Function, eARC, Auto Low Latency Mode, Variable Refresh Rate, HDMI Color Subsampling, HDMI Mode, Game Low Latency, Film Mode, Game HDR, Noise Reduction, Auto Game Mode, Color Temperature, 4:4:4 Color Space, Color Space Range, Picture Size and Position.
Expected: All 16 HDMI sub-settings items are listed and navigable.

Step 3: Toggle CEC On/Off.
Expected: CEC setting toggles correctly; CEC Function sub-option appears when CEC is On.

Step 4: Navigate to Picture Size and Position.
Expected: Picture adjustment controls appear.

Step 5: Verify AMC FreeSync logos appear in Gaming Menu when connected to compatible source.
Expected: FreeSync certification logos displayed in Gaming settings.
test_cases.expected_output: HDMI & Gaming menu contains all 16 required sub-settings. Each item is navigable and toggleable. CEC controls and FreeSync logos display correctly.

test_cases.title: Verify Bluetooth and Remote menu options
test_cases.description:
Step 1: Navigate to Settings > Bluetooth and Remote.
Expected: Bluetooth and Remote menu opens showing paired devices and options.

Step 2: Verify menu shows: paired remote, Headphones section, More Available Devices.
Expected: All sections present.

Step 3: Navigate to Headphones > More Available Devices.
Expected: BT device discovery list appears.

Step 4: Verify that if a remote is paired and TV is restarted from OSD menu, remote remains paired after restart.
Expected: Remote reconnects automatically after restart.

Step 5: Navigate to Gaming Controller sub-menu.
Expected: Gaming Controller options appear (Amazon Luna Controller, etc.).

Step 6: Select Amazon Luna Controller > verify Menu works as Settings.
Expected: Luna controller menu integration functions as expected.
test_cases.expected_output: Bluetooth/Remote menu shows all sections (paired remote, headphones, available devices, gaming controllers). Remote pairing persists through restart. Controller menus accessible.

test_cases.title: Verify Antenna Channels settings menu
test_cases.description:
Step 1: Navigate to Settings > Antenna Channels.
Expected: Antenna Channels menu opens showing all available options.

Step 2: Verify menu options include: Find Channels, Find New Channels, Hide Channels.
Expected: All channel management options present.

Step 3: Select Find Channels and verify available options within the wizard.
Expected: Channel scan wizard launches with frequency/type selection.

Step 4: Select Find New Channels and verify options.
Expected: Incremental channel scan options appear.

Step 5: Select 'Done' button after a scan operation.
Expected: Returns to the Antenna Channels menu and tuner is ready.

Step 6: Open Hide Channels.
Expected: Channel list appears with toggle options to show/hide individual channels.
test_cases.expected_output: Antenna Channels menu shows Find/Find New/Hide Channels options. Each sub-menu loads correctly. Done button returns to parent. Hide Channels allows per-channel toggling.

test_cases.title: Verify Closed Captions settings toggle and modes
test_cases.description:
Step 1: Navigate to Settings > Captions (or Accessibility > Captions).
Expected: Captions settings menu opens.

Step 2: Verify Closed Captions is OFF by default.
Expected: CC toggle shows OFF state on fresh/reset TV.

Step 3: Toggle Closed Captions ON.
Expected: CC toggle changes to ON; setting is persisted.

Step 4: Navigate to Analog Closed Captions sub-menu.
Expected: Analog CC options displayed (CC1, CC2, CC3, CC4, TEXT1-4).

Step 5: Navigate to Digital Closed Captions sub-menu.
Expected: Digital CC service options displayed.

Step 6: Toggle CC from remote CC button.
Expected: CC toggles on/off via dedicated remote button.

Step 7: Verify CC can be toggled using remote CC button.
Expected: Same toggle behavior as menu setting.
test_cases.expected_output: CC is OFF by default. Can be toggled ON/OFF from Settings and CC remote button. Analog and Digital CC sub-menus show correct options.

test_cases.title: Verify Closed Captions cross-input and application behavior
test_cases.description:
Step 1: Enable Closed Captions from Settings.
Expected: CC is ON globally.

Step 2: Verify CC setting is a Global Setting (applies across all inputs/apps).
Expected: CC state persists when switching inputs.

Step 3: Switch to Composite input with CC-enabled content.
Expected: Closed Captions appear on Composite input content.

Step 4: Switch to Tuner input with CC-enabled broadcast.
Expected: Closed Captions appear on Tuner/antenna content.

Step 5: Open a streaming application with CC-supported content.
Expected: Some applications follow the TV's CC setting.

Step 6: In an app with CC-supported content, press the CC button on remote.
Expected: App responds to CC key if content has CC support.

Step 7: Change Digital Style settings (font size, color, background).
Expected: CC display changes to reflect new style settings.

Step 8: Change CC setting from SmartCast app (mobile).
Expected: CC setting change is annunciated/reflected on the TV.
test_cases.expected_output: CC is a global setting persistent across inputs. Works on Composite, Tuner, and streaming apps. CC button works in apps. Digital Style changes apply. SmartCast app integration works.

test_cases.title: Verify Accessibility settings (TalkBack and Zoom)
test_cases.description:
Step 1: Navigate to Settings > Accessibility.
Expected: Accessibility menu opens showing TalkBack, Zoom Mode, Video Description options.

Step 2: Enable TalkBack.
Expected: TalkBack activates; all subsequent menu navigation is spoken aloud.

Step 3: Navigate through settings with TalkBack ON and adjust settings using basic remote.
Expected: TalkBack speaks all settings changes as they are adjusted.

Step 4: Change Menu Language to Spanish or French, then verify TalkBack behavior.
Expected: TalkBack is NOT supported on Spanish or French languages; feature is disabled or shows warning.

Step 5: Disable TalkBack.
Expected: Speech output stops; normal navigation resumes.

Step 6: Test TalkBack ON/OFF when no network is connected.
Expected: TalkBack functions without network dependency.

Step 7: Long-press the MENU key to enable Zoom navigation mode.
Expected: Zoom mode activates with confirmation dialog.

Step 8: Verify short-press of MENU key when Zoom is enabled.
Expected: MENU key still opens settings; Zoom remains active.

Step 9: Long-press MENU key again to disable Zoom.
Expected: Zoom mode deactivates with confirmation.

Step 10: Test Zoom Mode ON/OFF when no network is connected.
Expected: Zoom functions without network dependency.
test_cases.expected_output: TalkBack enables/disables correctly, speaks settings changes, doesn't work in Spanish/French, functions offline. Zoom mode activates/deactivates via long-press MENU, works offline.

test_cases.title: Verify Accessibility - Video Description settings
test_cases.description:
Step 1: Navigate to Settings > Accessibility > Video Description.
Expected: Video Description menu opens.

Step 2: Verify Video Description toggle is available.
Expected: Toggle present and functional.

Step 3: Verify Video Description option is removed for Spanish under Accessibility > Video Description (per Change List).
Expected: When language is set to Spanish, Video Description option is not shown or is disabled.

Step 4: Switch back to English and verify Video Description reappears.
Expected: Option returns when language is English.
test_cases.expected_output: Video Description is accessible under Accessibility settings. Not available in Spanish language mode per requirements.

test_cases.title: Verify Menu Language settings
test_cases.description:
Step 1: Navigate to Settings > System > Menu Language.
Expected: Menu Language settings screen opens showing available languages.

Step 2: Select Spanish as menu language.
Expected: All menu items, labels, and system text switch to Spanish immediately.

Step 3: Navigate through settings in Spanish to verify all strings are translated.
Expected: No English fallback text visible; all UI elements in Spanish.

Step 4: Switch to French.
Expected: All menu items switch to French.

Step 5: Switch back to English.
Expected: All menu items return to English.

Step 6: Verify Menu Language requirements (supported languages, font rendering, special characters).
Expected: All supported languages render correctly without truncation or encoding errors.
test_cases.expected_output: Menu Language switches correctly between English, Spanish, and French. All UI strings translate completely. Special characters render properly.

test_cases.title: Verify System menu and Power options
test_cases.description:
Step 1: Navigate to Settings > System.
Expected: System menu opens showing all system options.

Step 2: Verify Power option is present in System menu.
Expected: Power settings accessible (Quick Start, ECO mode options).

Step 3: Restart TV from OSD Menu while in ECO Mode.
Expected: TV powers off and powers on within 10 seconds.

Step 4: Restart TV from Reset Factory Settings > Restart option in ECO Mode.
Expected: TV restarts within expected timeframe.

Step 5: Verify Store Demo mode options (Demo 1, Demo 2).
Expected: Store Demo modes are accessible under System settings.

Step 6: Select Store Demo > Demo 1.
Expected: Demo mode 1 activates.

Step 7: Select Store Demo > Demo 2.
Expected: Demo mode 2 activates.
test_cases.expected_output: System menu shows Power option with ECO/Quick Start modes. Restart works from OSD and Reset menu. Store Demo modes 1 & 2 accessible and activatable.

test_cases.title: Verify Factory Reset sub-options (Reset Apps, Restart, Cancel)
test_cases.description:
Step 1: Navigate to Settings > System > Reset to Factory Settings.
Expected: Reset menu shows all options: Reset TV, Reset Apps, Restart, Cancel.

Step 2: Select 'Reset Apps'.
Expected: Apps are reset to default state; user settings and accounts preserved.

Step 3: Navigate back to Reset menu, select 'Restart'.
Expected: TV restarts (warm reboot) without factory reset.

Step 4: After restart, verify TV returns to Home screen (not OOBE).
Expected: TV boots to normal state; user data intact.

Step 5: Navigate to Reset menu, select 'Cancel'.
Expected: Dialog closes; no action taken; returns to Settings.
test_cases.expected_output: Reset Apps resets apps without full wipe. Restart performs warm reboot. Cancel dismisses dialog safely. Only Reset TV performs full factory reset.

test_cases.title: Verify Privacy & Legal menu and Viewing Data settings
test_cases.description:
Step 1: Navigate to Settings > Privacy & Legal.
Expected: Privacy & Legal menu option is accessible.

Step 2: Open Privacy & Legal and verify sub-options.
Expected: Shows Viewing Data, Advertising, and legal document links.

Step 3: Verify Advertising sub-menu.
Expected: Advertising menu appears with Limited Ad Tracking option.

Step 4: Focus on Limited Ad Tracking option.
Expected: Help/description text appears at bottom of menu explaining the option.

Step 5: After declining Viewing Data during OOBE, navigate to Settings > Privacy > Viewing Data.
Expected: Viewing Data toggle shows OFF state.

Step 6: Toggle Viewing Data ON from the menu.
Expected: Viewing Data setting changes to ON; opt-in confirmed.

Step 7: Verify Network > Terms of Service and policies link when OOBE and Network were skipped.
Expected: VIZIO Terms of Service and policies are accessible from Network settings even without completing OOBE.
test_cases.expected_output: Privacy & Legal menu accessible with Advertising/Viewing Data sub-options. Limited Ad Tracking shows description text. Viewing Data can be toggled post-OOBE. ToS accessible after OOBE skip.

test_cases.title: Verify OSD Menu launch and Input selection
test_cases.description:
Step 1: Select OSD Menu from Bluetooth Remote (BTRCU) by pressing GEAR/MENU button.
Expected: OSD Menu appears with list of options.

Step 2: Verify OSD Menu contents.
Expected: Menu shows expected items (Settings, Input, Picture Mode, etc.).

Step 3: Select 'Input' key on OSD Menu list.
Expected: Input selection screen appears showing all available inputs (HDMI 1-4, AV, Tuner, etc.).

Step 4: Launch Picture Mode Sidebar via PIC key on remote.
Expected: Picture Mode sidebar appears with available picture modes (Standard, Calibrated, Game, etc.).

Step 5: Select a different picture mode.
Expected: Picture mode changes immediately.
test_cases.expected_output: OSD Menu launches from BTRCU. Input selection accessible from OSD. PIC key launches Picture Mode sidebar with working mode selection.

test_cases.title: Verify OOBE Network skip and 'Set Up Later' messaging
test_cases.description:
Step 1: During OOBE, navigate to the Network connection popup/screen.
Expected: Network connection options appear.

Step 2: Tap 'Set Up Later' on the Network popup.
Expected: Updated text message confirms network will be set up later.

Step 3: Verify the text message content matches specification.
Expected: Correct wording displayed per UX requirements.

Step 4: Verify OOBE proceeds to next step after skipping network.
Expected: OOBE continues without network dependency.

Step 5: In OOBE 'Connect to a WiFi Network' screen, tap 'Skip Network' or 'Setup Network Later'.
Expected: Network step is bypassed; OOBE advances.
test_cases.expected_output: 'Set Up Later' and 'Skip Network' options work correctly with proper messaging. OOBE proceeds without requiring network connection.

test_cases.title: Verify TV Information access during OOBE
test_cases.description:
Step 1: Start OOBE (factory reset or first boot) and stop at 'Choose a Language' screen.
Expected: Language selection screen displayed.

Step 2: Press settings/menu button on 'Choose a Language' screen.
Expected: TV INFORMATION overlay appears showing model, firmware version, etc.

Step 3: Dismiss TV Information and proceed to 'Connect Your Network' screen.
Expected: Returns to OOBE flow.

Step 4: Connect network during OOBE, then press settings/menu button.
Expected: TV INFORMATION accessible with network-specific info (IP, MAC) now populated.

Step 5: Back out of OOBE from 'Connect Your Network' screen, press menu.
Expected: TV Information still accessible from backed-out state.
test_cases.expected_output: TV Information is accessible via Menu button at all OOBE stages. Shows system info before and after network connection.

test_cases.title: Verify Support menu access
test_cases.description:
Step 1: Navigate to Settings > Support (or System > Support).
Expected: Support menu opens.

Step 2: Verify support options are displayed (Check for Updates, System Information, Contact Support, etc.).
Expected: All support menu items present and navigable.

Step 3: Verify the GEAR/MENU button on remote opens settings consistently.
Expected: GEAR or MENU button always opens the Settings/Quick Settings regardless of current TV state (home, app, input).
test_cases.expected_output: Support menu accessible with all expected options. GEAR/MENU button consistently opens settings from any TV state.

rationale.TC-SYS-001: Consolidates 12 menu navigation/traversal TCs into single full-tree navigation test with parameterized sub-menu entries.
rationale.TC-SYS-002: Consolidates 4 System Information display TCs (different access contexts) into single TC testing all contexts.
rationale.TC-SYS-003: Consolidates 3 Factory Reset TCs (menu trigger + front panel trigger + Reset TV option) into single end-to-end reset validation.
rationale.TC-SYS-004: Maps to 1 sleep timer countdown/notification TC.
rationale.TC-SYS-006: Consolidates 5 menu timeout/dismissal TCs (60s timeout in Settings + Quick Settings + gaming controller non-interference) into single idle-behavior test.
rationale.TC-SYS-007: Consolidates 15 Quick Settings dismissal TCs (6 button types + timeout + re-open + channel/volume responses + pop-up interaction) via parameterized button loop.
rationale.TC-SYS-008: Consolidates 4 Quick Settings content/state TCs (online vs offline, general items, network shortcut) into single state-aware verification.
rationale.TC-SYS-009: Consolidates 13 Network menu UI TCs (SSID list, auto-rescan, selector persistence, rescan-only-when-active) — same 4 assertions repeated across 3+ model variants in manual suite.
rationale.TC-SYS-010: Consolidates 9 WiFi connection-via-menu TCs (2.4/5/6 GHz × WPA2/WPA3/SAE/Enhanced Open/Mixed) into parameterized band×encryption matrix.
rationale.TC-SYS-011: Consolidates 20 network switch/error TCs (8 'Choose Different Network' repeats × valid/invalid + disconnect diagnostic + error notifications) into single happy/unhappy path test.
rationale.TC-SYS-012: Consolidates 8 'Launch Network Settings from troubleshooting screen' TCs (same flow repeated from different entry points) into parameterized entry-point test.
rationale.TC-SYS-013: Consolidates 7 Captive Portal/SoftAP TCs (SSID listing + setup flow repeated across contexts + multi-platform SoftAP) into single captive portal flow test.
rationale.TC-SYS-014: Consolidates 10 Network Security Type / DHCP Settings TCs (menu text + security list + 3 DHCP modes × 2 model variants) into single menu validation.
rationale.TC-SYS-015: Consolidates 13 OOBE-from-menu TCs (6 'Launch OOBE from OSD' repeats + menu key during OOBE + Set Up Later messages + sidebar access) into single OOBE settings interaction test.
rationale.TC-SYS-016: Consolidates 18 HDMI & Gaming menu TCs (16 individual setting items + CEC toggle + FreeSync logos) into single menu-tree traversal with assertion per item.
rationale.TC-SYS-017: Consolidates 5 Bluetooth/Remote/Controller TCs (menu presence + headphones + remote persistence + gaming controllers) into single BT menu verification.
rationale.TC-SYS-018: Consolidates 5 Antenna Channels menu TCs (Find Channels + Find New + Hide + Done button + options list) into single channel settings workflow.
rationale.TC-SYS-019: Consolidates 7 CC toggle/mode TCs (ON/OFF, Analog, Digital, CC button × 2 remote variants, default state) into single CC settings test.
rationale.TC-SYS-020: Consolidates 9 CC cross-input/app TCs (Global Setting + Composite + Tuner + App follow + CC key in app + Digital Style + SmartCast app) into single cross-platform CC behavior test.
rationale.TC-SYS-021: Consolidates 8 Accessibility TCs (TalkBack ON/OFF + language restriction + offline + Zoom enable/disable via long-press + short-press behavior) into single a11y workflow.
rationale.TC-SYS-022: Consolidates 2 Video Description TCs (standard + Spanish removal per Change List) into single language-aware test.
rationale.TC-SYS-023: Consolidates 5 Menu Language TCs (English/Spanish/French switching + requirements verification) via language loop.
rationale.TC-SYS-024: Consolidates 5 System/Power/Store Demo TCs (power option + ECO restart × 2 + Store Demo 1 + Demo 2) into single system menu test.
rationale.TC-SYS-025: Consolidates 3 Factory Reset sub-option TCs (Reset Apps + Restart + Cancel) that test non-full-reset paths.
rationale.TC-SYS-026: Consolidates 7 Privacy/Legal/Advertising/Viewing Data TCs (menu access + advertising sub-menu + ad tracking text + viewing data toggle + ToS after skip) into single privacy settings test.
rationale.TC-SYS-027: Consolidates 3 OSD Menu/Input/Picture Mode TCs (OSD launch from BTRCU + Input selection + PIC key sidebar) into single OSD interaction test.
rationale.TC-SYS-028: Consolidates 4 OOBE network skip messaging TCs ('Set Up Later' text × 3 repeats + Skip Network) into single skip-flow verification.
rationale.TC-SYS-029: Consolidates 4 TV Information during OOBE TCs (Menu button on Choose Language + Connect Network + network connected + backed-out state) into single OOBE-info test.
rationale.TC-SYS-030: Consolidates 3 Support menu / GEAR button TCs (support menu access + GEAR button consistency × 2 remote variants).
